steps.title: Compare the two readings
steps.content: The passage first notes a common interpretation of Instant Mural: critics focus on Valdez and the social constraints women faced. But the passage says that reading leaves Sandoval's presence unexplained. The better conclusion must account for both collaborators, not just Valdez.

steps.title: Use the art context
steps.content: The passage then shifts to the art establishment's treatment of Chicano artists in 1974. Recognition was restricted to sociohistorical muralists, while nonmuralist Chicano artists such as Asco's members struggled to exhibit their work. This context points to constraint from aesthetic expectations placed on Chicano artists generally.

steps.title: Reject incomplete conclusions
steps.content: Choice A keeps the gender reading for Valdez and miscasts Sandoval's role as muralists' frustration. Choice B invents a critique of muralist depictions of Chicano culture. Choice C narrows the subject to female Chicano artists, so it still does not account for Sandoval.

steps.title: Account for all evidence
steps.content: Choice D fits the passage's alternative interpretation because it treats Instant Mural as a reflection on constraining aesthetic expectations placed on Chicano artists in general. That broader reading explains why both Valdez and Sandoval are taped to the wall. The answer is D.
